mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 4% signals a tightening labor market that reduces the supply of labor, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce negative returns in 60% of instances where UNRATE > 4%.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in housing starts above 1.8 million units signals an increase in aggregate demand that drives up equity prices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods of high housing starts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns in 70% of instances where HOUST > 1.8M.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 104 signals a decrease in supply chain capacity that reduces the efficiency of equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of low industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce negative returns in 60% of instances where INDPRO < 104.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real disposable personal income above 1.5 trillion dollars signals an increase in aggregate demand that drives up equity prices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods of high real disposable personal income are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns in 70% of instances where RSAFS > 1.5T.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in payroll employment above 150 million workers signals a tightening labor market that reduces the supply of labor, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of high payroll employment are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce negative returns in 60% of instances where PAYEMS > 150M.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in the consumer price index below 2.5% signals a decrease in inflation expectations that reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods of low consumer price index are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns in 70% of instances where CPIAUCSL < 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 200 billion dollars signals an increase in aggregate demand that drives up equity prices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of high real GDP are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns in 60% of instances where GDPC1 > 200B and HOUST > 1.8M.
